pattern: The probability telescopes to a constant 0.5 for any n >= 2; only n=1 is a special case.
hints: Work out small cases: when n=1 the first passenger has only one seat; when n=2 either the first person picks their own seat or the only remaining seat.
For n >= 3, notice that whenever the first passenger's chain of displacement ends, it resolves either on seat 1 or seat n with equal probability by symmetry.
The answer is 1 when n=1 and exactly 0.5 for every n >= 2 — no recursion or simulation is needed once you see the symmetry argument.

edge_inputs.description: single passenger — only one seat choice
two passengers — base case of the 0.5 result
small n to confirm constant probability
